heading: Assistance to producers

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: A producer may receive financial or technical assistance to conduct eligible activities on eligible land through a program contract entered into with the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program contract to be entered into with a producer to conduct eligible activities on eligible land, subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary may establish.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An eligible partner may submit to the Secretary, on behalf of producers, a bundle of applications for assistance under the program through program contracts to address a substantial portion of the conservation benefits to be achieved by the project, as defined in the partnership agreement.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: B Priority
children.text: The Secretary may give priority to applications described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to , the Secretary may make payments to a producer in an amount determined by the Secretary to be necessary to achieve the purposes of the program.section 3871d of this title

children.label: A
children.text: to producers participating in a project that addresses water quantity concerns and in an amount sufficient to encourage conversion from irrigated to dryland farming; and

children.label: B
children.text: to producers participating in a project that addresses water quality concerns and in an amount sufficient to encourage adoption of conservation practices and systems that improve nutrient management.

children.heading: Waiver authority
children.label: 3 Waiver authority
children.text: To assist in the implementation of the program, the Secretary may waive the applicability of the limitation in for producers if the Secretary determines that the waiver is necessary to fulfill the objectives of the program.section 1308–3a(b)(2) of title 7

children.heading: Funding through alternative funding arrangements or grant agreements
children.label: d Funding through alternative funding arrangements or grant agreements

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A partnership agreement entered into with an eligible partner may be funded through an alternative funding arrangement or grant in accordance with this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: use funding made available to carry out this subchapter to provide funding directly to the eligible partner; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide technical and administrative assistance, as mutually agreed by the parties; and

children.label: B
children.text: enter into not more than 15 alternative funding arrangements or grant agreements with 1 or more eligible partners each fiscal year.

children.label: I
children.text: benefit multiple producers; and

children.label: II
children.text: address natural resource concerns such as drought, wildfire, or water quality impairment on the land covered by the project;

children.label: ii
children.text: projects addressing natural resources concerns in coordination with producers, including the development and implementation of watershed, habitat, or other area restoration plans;

children.label: I
children.text: the provision of performance-based payments to producers; and

children.label: II
children.text: support for an environmental market; or

children.label: iv
children.text: other projects for which the Secretary determines that the goals and objectives of the program would be easier to achieve through the funding agreement under paragraph (1); and

children.label: i
children.text: the use of the funds awarded under paragraph (1);

children.label: ii
children.text: any subcontracts awarded;

children.label: iii
children.text: the producers receiving funding through the funding agreement under paragraph (1);

children.label: I
children.text: the progress made by the project in addressing each natural resource concern defined in the funding agreement under paragraph (1), including in a quantified form to the extent practicable; and

children.label: II
children.text: as appropriate, other outcomes of the project; and

children.label: v
children.text: any other reporting data the Secretary determines are necessary to ensure compliance with the program rules.
